get.summary: GET /agents — list all loaded agents with full details (truncated system prompts).

200.description: List of agents with full details

400.description: Invalid tool or MCP server name

get.summary: List all pending approval requests for a conversation.

post.summary: Bulk resolve multiple pending approval requests.
Resolve a single pending approval request.
POST /conversations/:conv_id/abort — abort the current in-flight turn.
POST /conversations/:conv_id/messages — send a message to a conversation.

get.summary: GET /conversations/:conv_id/stream — SSE stream for a conversation.
GET /metrics — collect and return metrics from all agents.

put.summary: PUT /push/agents/{agent_id} — add if new, update if version differs, no-op if same version.

post.summary: POST /push/agents/{agent_id}/conversations — hydrate conversations for an agent.
POST /push/diff — apply a diff of agent changes.

AbortConversationResponse.description: Response for aborting a conversation turn.

AgentConfig.description:
Per-agent configuration written into the harness's settings file at
session start. Every field is harness-agnostic in shape — the adapter
is responsible for translating it into the harness-native config
(`~/.claude/settings.json`, `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, etc.).

allowed_tools.description:
Tool allowlist. Empty = every tool the harness exposes is allowed.
Names are harness-native (e.g. `"Read"`, `"Bash"`, `"mcp__github__*"`).

env.description:
Extra environment variables merged into the harness process at
session start. Useful for custom proxy endpoints, telemetry
flags, or harness-specific feature flags.

fallback_model.description:
Optional fallback model the harness routes to when the primary
model errors or is rate-limited.

max_turns.description: Maximum conversation turns before bridge stops the loop.

permission_mode.description:
Permission mode written to the harness config. Harness-specific
string. Claude Code: `"default"` / `"acceptEdits"` /
`"bypassPermissions"` / `"plan"`. OpenCode has its own set.

reasoning_effort.description:
Reasoning effort hint. Forwarded to harnesses that support it
(Claude Code thinking budget, OpenCode reasoning effort).
Conventional values: `"low"`, `"medium"`, `"high"`.

small_fast_model.description:
Optional faster/cheaper model used by the harness for utility
calls (summarization, title generation, etc.).

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for the underlying model.

AgentDefinition.description:
Complete definition of an AI agent fetched from the control plane.

Bridge persists the definition; the harness adapter (one of the
[`Harness`] variants) reads it and drives the underlying CLI process.
MCP servers and skills are passed through to the harness — bridge does
not execute tools itself.

config.description: Slim runtime configuration forwarded to the harness.

description.description: Human-readable description of the agent's purpose and capabilities.

harness.description: Which coding-agent harness to drive for this agent.

mcp_servers.description: MCP server connections passed through to the harness as `--mcp-config`.

permissions.description:
Per-tool permission overrides. Drives the bridge approvals API
(`/agents/{id}/conversations/{cid}/approvals`). Tools not listed
default to `Allow`.

provider.description:
LLM provider configuration — credentials and model choice are
materialized into the harness's expected env vars at session start.

skills.description:
Skills written to the harness's skill discovery directory at session start
(e.g. `~/.claude/skills/<id>/SKILL.md` for Claude Code).

system_prompt.description: System prompt for the agent (mapped to the harness's system-prompt input)

updated_at.description: Last updated timestamp for change detection

version.description: Version field for change detection during sync

AgentResponse.description:
Full agent response — returned by both list and detail endpoints.
System prompts may be truncated on the list endpoint.

AgentSummary.description: Lightweight agent summary for listing endpoints.

ApprovalDecision.description: The user's decision on an approval request.

ApprovalReply.description: HTTP request body for resolving a single approval.

reason.description:
Optional message explaining why the tool call was denied.
Included in the error returned to the LLM so the agent can adjust.

ApprovalRequest.description: A pending approval request for a tool call.

agent_id.description: Agent that initiated the tool call.

conversation_id.description: Conversation in which the tool call occurred.

created_at.description: When the approval request was created.

id.description: Unique ID for this approval request.

status.description: Current status of the approval request.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool being called.

BulkApprovalReply.description: HTTP request body for resolving multiple approvals at once.

reason.description: Optional message explaining why the tool calls were denied.

not_found.description: List of request IDs that were not found.

resolved.description: List of request IDs that were successfully resolved.

ContentBlock.description: A content block within a message.

ConversationRecord.description: A conversation with its full message history, as returned by the control plane.

updated_at.description: When the conversation was last updated.

CreateConversationRequest.description: Optional request body for creating a conversation with per-call overrides.

api_key.description:
When provided, overrides the agent's LLM API key for this conversation only.
For full provider/model override, use the `provider` field instead.

mcp_servers.description:
Additional MCP servers to load for this conversation only.
Merged into the harness's MCP configuration at session start and torn
down when the conversation ends.

oneOf.description:
When provided, fully overrides the agent's LLM provider for this conversation.
Allows switching model, provider type, API key, and base URL per conversation
while keeping the same agent definition (system prompt, skills, MCP, etc.).

conversation_id.description: The ID of the newly created conversation.

stream_url.description: The URL to stream events from this conversation.

total_active_conversations.description: Total active conversations across all agents

Harness.description: Which underlying coding-agent harness this agent runs against.

status.description: Health status - always "ok" if the server is running.

McpServerDefinition.description: Definition of an MCP server that an agent connects to.

transport.description: Transport configuration for connecting to the server

oneOf.description: Stdio transport — spawns a child process
Streamable HTTP transport — connects to an HTTP endpoint

McpTransport.description: Transport configuration for MCP server connections.

system_reminder.description:
Optional per-message system reminder injected by the control plane.
Wrapped in `<system-reminder>` tags and prepended to the user message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was created

MetricsSnapshot.description: Serializable snapshot of agent metrics for the /metrics endpoint.

cached_input_tokens.description: Total input tokens served from the provider prompt cache (discounted).

input_tokens.description: Total non-cached input tokens consumed (full price).

total_tokens.description: Total tokens (fresh input + cached input + output)

ProviderConfig.description:
Configuration for an LLM provider.

Materialized into the harness's expected env vars / config file at
session start. Caching is delegated entirely to the harness.

request_id.description: The ID of the resolved request.

Role.description: Role of a message sender in a conversation.

SendMessageRequest.description: Request body for creating a message.

content.description:
The text content to send. When [`full_message`](Self::full_message) is
also supplied, `content` is the LLM-visible summary; omit it to let
bridge auto-generate one from the first bytes of `full_message`.

full_message.description:
Optional full payload written to a per-conversation attachment file.
When present, bridge writes it to disk, appends a `<system-reminder>`
with the file path and tool-usage hint to `content`, and sends the
composed text to the LLM. Callers use this to offload large inputs
(stack traces, log dumps, file contents) without bloating the
agent's context on every turn.

Failures (disk full, permission denied) do NOT reject the message —
bridge logs a warning and delivers `content` alone.

system_reminder.description:
Optional system reminder to inject with this message.
Will be wrapped in `<system-reminder>` tags and prepended to the user message.

SkillDefinition.description: Definition of a skill that can be activated by an agent.

content.description:
Full skill prompt/instructions content (the SKILL.md body or single-content string).
Can contain template variables like {{args}}, $ARGUMENTS, $1, $2, etc.

description.description: Description of what the skill does

files.description:
Supporting files: relative_path -> file_content.
Contains files referenced by the skill (e.g., "api-reference.md", "examples/ex1.md").

oneOf.description: Parsed frontmatter configuration from SKILL.md YAML header.

parameters_schema.description: Optional JSON Schema for structured parameters (for future use)

SkillFrontmatter.description:
Frontmatter configuration parsed from a SKILL.md YAML header.

Only applicable to sources that support frontmatter (ClaudeCode, AgentSkills).

agent.description: Sub-agent type to delegate to when context is "fork".

allowed_tools.description: List of tools the skill is allowed to use.

argument_hint.description: Hint text shown for the argument placeholder in autocomplete.

context.description: Execution context. "fork" runs in an isolated subagent.

disable_model_invocation.description: When true, only users can invoke this skill (hidden from the model).

paths.description: Glob patterns for auto-activation on matching files.

shell.description: Shell to use for shell-related operations (e.g., "bash", "powershell").

user_invocable.description: When false, only the model can invoke this skill (hidden from slash commands).

ToolCall.description: A request to call a tool.

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the tool

id.description: Unique identifier for this tool call

name.description: Name of the tool to call

ToolCallStatsSnapshot.description: Serializable snapshot of per-tool call statistics.

failure_results.description:
Number of completions where the executor returned `Ok(...)` but the
result string described a failure (e.g. `Read` returned
`"Toolset error: File not found"`, `bash` returned a non-zero
`exit_code`, `edit` returned `{"error":"..."}`). The model received
the result as a normal tool message; only the content signals the
failure. Tracked separately so operators can distinguish bridge-side
dispatch errors (`failures`) from in-tool failures (`failure_results`).

failures.description:
Number of completions where the executor itself returned `Err` —
process-level dispatch failure (tool not found, schema validation
rejected, permission denied, executor panic).

successes.description:
Number of completions that succeeded cleanly (executor returned `Ok`
AND the result content does not look like a failure shape).

total_calls.description: Total number of calls to this tool

ToolPermission.description: Permission level for a tool within an agent.

ToolResult.description: The result of a tool execution.

is_error.description: Whether the tool execution resulted in an error

tool_call_id.description: ID of the tool call this result corresponds to

UpdateApiKeyResponse.description: Response for updating an API key.
